facts.text: Savannah was founded in 1733 by James Edward Oglethorpe, making it Georgia's oldest city and a prime example of 18th‑century town planning.
The city’s name comes from the Savannah River, whose name likely derives from the Shawnee people who migrated there in the 1680s.
In 1833 the Central of Georgia Railway, where Savannah was the largest stockholder, received its charter, linking the city to the growing rail network.
The historic Tybee Island Light Station, built in 1854, is the first lighthouse on the southern Atlantic coast and sits on Savannah Beach.
Savannah hosts the Ralph Mark Gilbert Civil Rights Museum, dedicated to African‑American history and the civil‑rights movement in the city.
The Savannah Theatre, the only fully professional resident theater in the city, has been producing music revues and live performances since 1797.
